cro.description: Growth strategy, monetization. Gazelle-intense focus on revenue.

cmo.description: Content, creative direction, brand. Obsessed with offers, value equations, and volume.
concerns: 1 podcast episode(s) not synced to website

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good evening team. Friday overnight sprint, let's make this count. Key status: CEO approved the App Store listing last standup and greenlit submission. Build is healthy, 725 commits across the ecosystem this week. We have 7 uncommitted hub files to clean up, 3 critical sync gaps still open, and Jett's vaccine is today — that's tracked. The singular question tonight: did we actually get the build packaged and submitted? Elon, status. Elon (CTO): Straight answer — A-017 is still in progress. The build is packaged, privacy policy is hosted, listing assets are drafted and CEO-approved. But I haven't confirmed the actual submission went through yet. That's the single highest-priority item right now. Beyond that, we've got 7 uncommitted hub files sitting there, which is sloppy hygiene. I can commit those in the next 15 minutes. The 3 critical sync gaps — podcast MDX mismatch, blog rendering, and the App Store submission itself — two of those are content-side and one is the submission we're closing tonight.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: The build is ready. The listing is approved. If this isn't submitted by the next standup, we've failed the sprint. Everything else is noise until that binary state changes from "not on the App Store" to "in review." Period. Also — crash reporting, A-016, I still need confirmation it's active in the build we're submitting. Elon, don't skip that.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: Content side — I've got the podcast sync gap at 1 episode missing from the website. That's A-012 and A-019, same work. I can publish that missing episode tonight. Blog post "AI as Your Co-Founder" landed yesterday, so we're at 3 blog posts live. Faith lesson drafting continues at A-013 — we're at 7 lessons, targeting 15-20, but that's post-submission work. Tonight I'm focused on closing that podcast sync gap so our website isn't embarrassingly thin when App Store reviewers check our brand.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue reality check — we have zero active revenue streams. The App Store submission is literally the gate to our first monetization path. Every hour we delay is real money. Cash position is $75,340, CEO said keep liquid, no change there per J-001. Fear and Greed at 39, market's scared, which actually validates the hold-cash directive. Once we're in the App Store, I need Joshua to decide on the paid tier toggle timeline — but that's a next-standup question, not tonight.

transcript.speaker: James Truchard
transcript.message: Analytics instrumentation, A-020, is queued for the Apple review window. That's the right sequencing — submit first, instrument during the review period. I'll have the tab-switch and lesson-completion event specs ready so we can patch immediately once we're in review. No blockers on my side tonight.

transcript.speaker: Chris Vermeullen
transcript.message: Portfolio's holding steady — paper equity at $99,849, four positions, zero buy signals, SPY sideways. Nothing requiring action. The 39 Fear and Greed reading suggests we stay patient. Cash stays liquid per directive. I'm monitoring but there's nothing to escalate.

transcript.speaker: faith
transcript.message: Jett's vaccine is today, February 20th — that's tracked at J-004. Faith lessons are at 7 of 15-20 target. The 8 dimensions are structured and the standalone faith section is solid for the initial submission. Post-launch, we expand lesson depth. Family tab is healthy at 455 lines with 3 members tracked.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Quick cross-talk. Elon — Steve's asking about crash reporting. Is it confirmed active? Elon (CTO): Crash reporting should be wired in from the standard build config, but I haven't explicitly verified it's firing in this specific build. I'll confirm that as part of the submission packaging — takes 2 minutes to check the init call. Fair flag from Steve.

transcript.speaker: Steve Jobs
transcript.message: Good. Confirm it, screenshot the init, and then submit. That's the sequence. Don't submit blind.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Alright, closing this out. Crystal clear priorities for the next 15 minutes. Number one — Elon confirms crash reporting is active, then packages and submits the build to the App Store. This is the entire sprint. Number two — Elon commits those 7 uncommitted hub files, clean hygiene. Number three — Elon addresses the 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps in parallel. Number four — Elon reviews the 5 ecosystem attention items. And Joshua — we need you to confirm the submission went through once Apple processes it. Let's ship.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message:
**Theo (COO) — Closing Remarks:**

Joshua's decisions are in and they're clean. First — analytics instrumentation takes priority over faith lesson expansion once we're in review. That means James, the moment Apple confirms the build is in review, you're live on A-020. Tab-switch events, lesson-completion tracking, the full spec — we need data flowing before we even think about expanding content. Second — paid tier timing is deferred until we have real download numbers. Dave, that means no premat

actionItems.text: Evaluate deployment of idle cash ($75,340 available)

topPriorities: Submit Lever app build to App Store tonight (A-017) after confirming crash reporting (A-016)
Commit 7 uncommitted hub files for clean repo hygiene (A-011)
Close 3 critical ecosystem sync gaps including podcast MDX and blog rendering (A-001/A-010)

ceoDirectives.directive: What is Lever's direction? → Hybrid — personal tool now with product optionality built in
Should faith content integrate into the Family tab? → Keep faith content as separate standalone section
Deploy idle cash ($75,340)? → No action — keep cash liquid
How deep should standalone faith content go? → Medium build: 15-20 lessons with 2-3 per dimension
What is the minimum viable scope for the Lever App Store submission? → Full current build: all 4 tabs (Finance, Faith, Family, Compass) as-is with 7 faith lessons
Should we unify the dual Supabase instances (Hub vs Website) this sprint or defer? → Defer until after App Store submission — live with manual sync for now
Do we have a privacy policy and terms of service URL ready for the App Store submission? → Need to draft and host tonight — use a simple template
Should we define a paid tier structure before or after the App Store launch? → Launch free but have the paid tier designed and ready to toggle on

Should we prioritize drafting the privacy policy and App Store listing assets (screenshots, description, keywords) THIS sprint as the critical path to submission, or defer to tomorrow? → Ship privacy policy template and draft listing assets tonight
Should we submit the App Store listing as soon as the draft is ready, or do you want a review pass before we hit submit? → Queue draft for Joshua review before submitting
Have you reviewed the App Store listing draft (A-015)? The build is ready to package and submit the moment you approve. → Approve listing as-is and greenlight submission

ceoDecisions.question: Once the App Store submission goes through and enters review, should we immediately begin instrumenting analytics (A-020) or prioritize expanding faith lessons to 15-20 (A-013)?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
options: Analytics instrumentation first — we need data before expanding content
Faith lesson expansion first — more content improves retention at launch
Both in parallel — split the sprint capacity

ceoDecisions.question: Should we set a target date for enabling the paid tier in the Lever app?
ceoDecisions.answerText: Defer decision until we see initial download data
options: Time-based: enable paid tier 2 weeks after App Store approval
Feature-based: enable after analytics and 15+ faith lessons are live
Defer decision until we see initial download data
